title: Issue from v1.29.0 (previous versions working well)
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Run in Gitlab CI with the image `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.29.0`. I tried multiple version from `v1.29.0` to `v1.32.0` and still the same issue.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Using push stage in Gitlab CI
```
buildah:
  stage: push
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.29.0
  cache: []
  before_script:
    - dnf install -y curl jq python3 python3-pip
    - pip install --no-cache-dir awscli
    - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_REGION | buildah login --username AWS --password-stdin $DOCKER_REGISTRY
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
time="2023-11-28T04:00:27Z" level=error msg="parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
time="2023-11-28T04:00:27Z" level=error msg="(Unable to determine exit status)"
Exception ignored in: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='<stdout>' mode='w' encoding='utf-8'>
BrokenPipeError: [Errno 32] Broken pipe
```

comments.body: @oo00spy00oo Could you please try `--security-opt=seccomp=unconfined` and `--privileged` I think it could be happening because of changes in default seccomp profile which changed with release, and if it works then we can close down on the needed policy for Gitlab CI ?

> @oo00spy00oo Could you please try `--security-opt=seccomp=unconfined` and `--privileged` I think it could be happening because of changes in default seccomp profile which changed with release, and if it works then we can close down on the needed policy for Gitlab CI ?

I tried to add it following your comment, but nothing happened!

See this issue https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4563

It's apparently intended and there's no intention to fix.

@sjdrc can you explain what is intended here? As I understand the intention of `buildah` is to be able to build images without privileged demons. So what is going on here, and why it is not possible to do without this `unshare(CLONE_USER)` stuff?
`unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER)` is not something "privileged". It just happens that unlike other containerization solutions, [Docker does not allow it by default](https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/42441).
@slonopotamus should GitLab CI switch to `podman` for running containers?
